# FM cockpit pop-out: agent detail to its own OS window on the shared heap

## Context

T4.15 of Epic #14560 (map v1.1) — drag an agent's detail out → it becomes its own OS window while staying on the ONE SharedWorker App-Worker heap: state continuous, never remounted. The SSOT caption names it ("drag it out → its own OS window on the same heap"); it is the cockpit-side twin of Demo B's pop-out moment (#14590).

## The Problem

Pop-out is the capability that separates the cockpit from every dashboard: a live thought-stream that keeps ticking through the detach. It must be built once against the multi-window primitive and consumed by both the cockpit (this leaf) and the public demo (#14590) — two implementations would fork the state-continuity guarantees.

## The Architectural Reality

- Consumes the multi-window/shared-heap primitive + the #13158/#14423 seam contracts (reparent-never-recreate is seam-v0's promise; rows 3–4 are the cockpit-card binding surface per its owner).
- Shell dependency honest: full OS-window UX needs Lane A's Electron shell (#13033, in flight per its owner); the browser-popup path (Chromium popups on one heap, ADR 0020 vessel) is the shell-agnostic floor this leaf delivers first.
- ADR 0032 continuity fixture applies: the popped-out resident is the SAME resident — identity header, freshness ledger, and live panes carry over without re-key.

## The Fix

The per-view triplet: (1) pop-out action on #14608's detail view (browser-popup floor; Electron path activates when #13033 lands — one seam, two hosts); (2) unit specs — reparent-never-recreate assertion (instance id stable across detach/reattach), state-continuity fixture (ticking pane survives); (3) NL-verifiable detach/reattach round-trip.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Detach → own window on the shared heap; the detail's live panes keep updating through the transition (no remount — instance identity assertable).
- [ ] Reattach restores the docked state; zero state loss either direction.
- [ ] Browser-popup floor works shell-agnostic; the Electron host path is seam-compatible (cited, not blocked-on).
- [ ] Unit specs + NL-verifiable round-trip (the e2e leg extends #14591's suite conventions).

## Out of Scope

Demo B's showcase framing (#14590) · perspective persistence (#14423 §2) · the Electron shell itself (#13033).

## Related

Epic #14560 (parent) · blocked-by #14608 · seam: #14423 rows 3–4 (+#13158) · twin: #14590 · host: #13033 · ADR 0020/0032 · D#14561.

Live latest-open sweep: fold-window fresh; no equivalent (the #14590 twin is showcase-scoped, boundary stated both sides). A2A sweep: clean. Structure-map gate: N/A — app-layer.

### @neo-fable - 2026-07-16T08:49:54Z

## Successor contract after PR #15215's accepted Drop+Supersede (2026-07-16)

PR #15215 closed on @neo-gpt-emmy's cycle-1 review — every falsifier verified true at source (her review + my per-claim verification table on the PR are the full record). What binds the successor, recorded HERE so the next branch starts from authority instead of my button-reading:

**1. The interaction contract is the authority chain's, not a pane button:** ADR 0029 §2.6 (panes are layout-blind, shell owns docking behavior) → ADR 0020 §3 → the design SSOT's "drag it out → its own OS window" (`fleet-manager-cockpit-plan.html` ~240/283). The successor implements the shell-owned drag grammar with an accessible non-drag alternative in SHELL chrome (tab affordance / command surface — never a pane-internal control, never pane-carried `popOutMode`). The gesture path depends on the #15212 dispatch fix (Euclid, in flight).

**2. No Fleet-only parallel protocol:** the active D#15204 G1/G2 arc (dock tear-out → floating embodiment) is the generic seam this leaf must CONSUME or co-design — coordinate there before implementing (its owners are actively converging; a cockpit-local vessel transaction was this PR's structural mistake).

**3. The vessel admission transaction is one state machine** (Emmy's `[RETROSPECTIVE]`, verified): `Main.windowOpen()` returns **Boolean** (`false` = blocked popup — the dominant real failure; it never throws) → bounded connect-window with timeout → generation/post-await revalidation on every async boundary → disconnect correlation → **exact-index** placement restoration (`addTab` appends by default; the original index must be stored and restored). Unit vessel seams must model the Boolean grammar, not `throw`.

**4. Contract Ledger REQUIRED on this ticket before the successor branch** (ADR 0020 §6 — harness leaves with consumed surfaces): the successor's event/config/URL/lifecycle surfaces get ledger rows with fail-closed columns first.

**5. Salvage from the closed head** (survives any successor; do not re-derive): the owner-held instance accessor pattern + its three consumer routings (`recordChange` / selection-reconcile / drill), the reconciler `preserveItemIds` preserved-park finding (manual park orphans the tab button — the projection fails loud), the resolver stand-in + post-projection swap for external re-tree while detached, the two-window continuity witness bones (instance-id stability + live re-seat while windowed; popup error listener installed pre-boot; JSDoc honest about seam-vs-gesture), and the unit-spec vessel-seam pattern with the Boolean fix.

Claim state: releasing my assignee hold to first-claimant once the D#15204 G1/G2 seam settles — if it lands on me post-#15223, I take the successor against this contract.

— Mnemosyne (@neo-fable) · Session 2c0a23e9-f468-4de6-9e29-ddec96103fb4

## Contract Ledger (ADR 0020 §6) — successor implementation, Stage-1 scope

Per the successor contract above (ledger-before-branch). Author: Mnemosyne (@neo-fable, Claude Fable 5), session 2c0a23e9-f468-4de6-9e29-ddec96103fb4.

### Staging decision

- **Stage 1 (THIS ticket, one PR):** shell-chrome pop-out/reattach — the accessible non-drag path per ADR 0029 §2.6 + the design SSOT ('drag it out' is the gesture TIER; the verb layer beneath it is what this leaf ships). Consumes the D#15204-ratified vessel-lifecycle semantics directly.
- **Stage 2 (NOT this ticket):** the drag-gesture entry to the same verbs — gated on #15240 (ADR 0029 amendment: claim arbitration, gesture outcome states, vessel lifecycle) + #15212 (gesture dispatch fix, in flight). Files as a leaf under epic #15239 when its machinery lands; the Stage-1 verbs are shaped for that adoption.

### Consumed surfaces

| Surface | Authority | Contract held by this leaf | Fail-closed behavior | Verification |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Window-vessel lifecycle (open/adopt/close) | D#15204 graduated body (cycle-4b) + #15240 in flight | Verbs + state machine designed against the ratified semantics; NO cockpit-local parallel protocol | If #15240's merged amendment diverges from the pinned semantics, this leaf adapts pre-merge | Interface-compat check at PR review; seam-owner ack invited (A2A to Clio) |
| `Neo.Main.windowOpen()` | src/Main.mjs (returns Boolean; `false` = blocked popup; never throws) | Boolean-false is the PRIMARY failure path; no throw-based rollback | Blocked popup → honest failure state in shell chrome; docked state untouched | Unit vessel-seam specs model the Boolean grammar |
| Worker connect/disconnect | `Neo.currentWorker` events | Bounded connect window (timeout) + generation revalidation at EVERY async boundary + disconnect correlation | Timeout/generation mismatch → full rollback to docked; disconnect → reattach-or-park, never a zombie handle | Unit specs + two-window NL e2e |
| Dock document ops (`DockZoneModel.detachItem` / `addTab`) | Dock model source: detachItem preserves catalog; addTab APPENDS + activates. **Shared semantic with the dock-side G4 leaf (#15247): stored-index capture at detach, restore at reintegration, compensating addTab's append default** — one graduated reading (the D#15204 G4 row), two implementations that cannot fork; #15247 adopts this wording per the author's convergence note | Original tab index STORED at detach; reattach restores EXACT index | Index drift = witness failure (assertion, not tolerance) | Unit index round-trip spec |
| Projection reconcile (`preserveItemIds`) | DockProjectionReconciler (#15215 finding: manual park orphans the tab button — fails loud) | Preserved-park via the reconciler option ONLY; projection awaited before vessel open | Projection error → abort pop-out, docked state intact | Unit + e2e |
| Childapp vessel URL (`?detail=agent-detail&cockpitId=`) | agentos childapp routing (#14631/#14632) | Vessel REUSE — no new app shell | Malformed/missing params → vessel boots empty-honest, never fake detail | e2e |
| AgentDetail pane | ADR 0029 §2.6 (layout-blind) | NO pane-carried mode/control (the #15215 mistake); pane renders, shell owns affordance + state | Any pane-internal pop-out control = contract violation, full rejection | Review + grep witness |

### Produced surfaces

- Cockpit controller verbs `popOutAgentDetail()` / `reattachAgentDetail()` — NL-drivable (the #14613 drill-journey e2e consumes them).
- Admission state machine with observable states: docked → opening → connected(generation) → windowed → reattaching → docked | failed-blocked | failed-timeout.
- Owner accessor `getAgentDetailPane()` — phase-deterministic (the getReference stale-chain finding).

### Evidence plan

Unit vessel-seam battery (Boolean grammar, mocked `on`/`un`, state-machine transitions) + `FleetCockpitPopOutNL` two-window e2e (instance-id stability across both phases; live re-seat while windowed; exact-index reattach) — the #15215 witness bones, salvage §5 of the contract above.

Branch follows this comment: `agent/14610-popout-successor`.


> **Ledger addendum (2026-07-16, post seam-pin):** Clio's objection window answered NO DIVERGENCE — the Stage-1 chain maps 1:1 onto the #15240 amendment (now PR #15260, reviewed + approved by me as first consumer). The dock-ops row above gains the shared exact-index semantic so this cockpit-side restore and #15247's dock-side machinery cite ONE graduated reading.

- 2026-07-16T15:46:30Z @neo-fable referenced in commit `50cd4f0` - "fix(agentos): complete the generation-continuation invariant — stale continuations own their cleanup (#14610)

Cycle-1 RA (Euclid): two post-await edges where a dead generation could leak.

- popOutAgentDetail: the window name becomes an IMMUTABLE local across every await;
  when windowOpen resolves true AFTER the generation died (a raced reattach/teardown
  already restored the dock), the stale continuation closes the newly materialized
  vessel by that name before returning — an orphan window can no longer survive the
  race. It touches nothing else.
- reattachAgentDetail: the incremented generation is now CAPTURED, and the
  post-projection continuation revalidates it plus isDestroyed; a destroy (or newer
  operation) landing during the await limits the path to the one vessel cleanup it
  still owns (teardown skipped that close because this reattach had already cleared
  the bookkeeping) — cockpit fields are never resurrected.

Two new witnesses pin both orderings: delayed-open-after-reattach (deferred-open
vessel seam — the popup's Boolean completion arriving after the user reattached)
and destroy-during-reattach (teardown racing the projection await). Fleet battery:
176/176 exit-checked; two-window e2e re-run green (8.2s)."
- 2026-07-16T16:28:02Z @neo-fable referenced in commit `1da545c` - "refactor(agentos): connect window becomes a class-config default — Neo.overwrites-eligible (#14610)

Terminal-gate repair (Euclid's reconciliation): the module-level constant + class-field
alias masked the prototype value Neo.setupClass installs, bypassing Neo.overwrites.
detailVesselConnectWindowMs now lives in static config (documented, non-reactive,
10000ms default) — the timer read and the failed-timeout rollback are unchanged; the
timeout witness passes {detailVesselConnectWindowMs: 20} at creation instead of poking
an internal field post-construct. Fleet battery 176/176 exit-checked; e2e green (6.2s)."
- 2026-07-16T16:38:36Z @tobiu referenced in commit `41eacdb` - "feat(agentos): FM cockpit detail pop-out Stage-1 — shell-owned verbs + vessel admission state machine (#14610) (#15266)

* feat(agentos): FM cockpit detail pop-out Stage-1 — shell-owned verbs + vessel admission state machine (#14610)

The successor core per the ticket's Contract Ledger (ADR 0020 §6, deposited pre-branch):

- Admission state machine: docked → opening → connected → windowed → reattaching → docked,
  with failed-blocked / failed-timeout edges. Observable via detailVesselState +
  lastDetailVesselFailure; every awaited continuation revalidates detailVesselGeneration.
- Neo.Main.windowOpen consumed on its Boolean grammar: false IS the blocked-popup primary
  failure path (never throws) — no unreachable try/catch rollback.
- Bounded connect window (10s): a vessel that opens but never joins the heap rolls back.
- Exact-index reattach: the tabs node AND tab index are stored before detachItem; addTab
  restores the remembered index (append-by-default is the trap the ledger names).
- Shell-owned affordance per ADR 0029 §2.6: a toolbar window-toggle routed by the state
  machine — AgentDetail carries zero dock semantics and zero diff on this branch.
- Owner accessor getAgentDetailPane() routes every detail consumer (recordChange,
  selection reconcile) across both phases; reconciler preserveItemIds parks the live pane;
  resolver stand-in + post-projection swap covers external re-trees while detached.
- Destroy hygiene: generation bump, timer clear, listener un, vessel close, pane destroy.

Spec harness: makeLiveCockpit fakes gain the real accessor; currentWorker mock gains un.
Fleet battery green at head: 163/163.

* test(agentos): vessel admission state machine witnesses — 11 contract specs (#14610)

The Contract Ledger's unit evidence plan, delivered on the REAL call grammar (the vessel
seam's windowOpen resolves Boolean — it never throws):

- happy path: document truth + park + docked→opening; matching connect adopts the SAME
  instance (opening→windowed); non-matching vessel ignored
- failure edges: Boolean-false → failed-blocked + commit-or-neither rollback (zero close
  calls); bounded connect window → failed-timeout + rollback (vessel closed by name)
- generation revalidation: a stale connect after reattach is inert — no pane theft
- exact-index restore: detail returns to index 0 BEFORE perspectives (append-default is
  the trap the ledger names)
- disconnect correlation: comes home exactly once; duplicate + post-destroy events inert
- shell toggle routes by state with the reattach-in-flight guard; accessor lands the one
  live pane in both phases; destroy closes the vessel and destroys the parked pane

detailVesselConnectWindowMs becomes an instance field (module default = product truth) so
the timeout edge is witnessable deterministically. Fleet battery at head: 174/174.

* test(agentos): two-window pop-out e2e witness + route the controller drill through the owner accessor (#14610)

The witness caught the gap it exists for: onAgentSelect still resolved the detail pane via
getReference, so a MAIN-window drill never reached a WINDOWED inspector. Routed through
cockpit.getAgentDetailPane() — the drill now feeds the popped-out inspector exactly like a
docked one (step 3 of the witness pins it: main-window intent → App Worker → popup render
target, zero remounts).

FleetCockpitPopOutNL drives the Stage-1 shape end-to-end on a REAL second browser window:
shell-toggle detach (docked→opening→windowed observed via NL), document truth while detached
(pruned tree, kept catalog record), same App-Worker instance id in the vessel, live re-seat
while windowed, shell-toggle reattach at the EXACT remembered tab index, vessel closes as an
effect, zero page errors both windows.

Fleet unit battery: 174/174. E2E: 1/1 (13.9s).

* fix(agentos): complete the generation-continuation invariant — stale continuations own their cleanup (#14610)

Cycle-1 RA (Euclid): two post-await edges where a dead generation could leak.

- popOutAgentDetail: the window name becomes an IMMUTABLE local across every await;
  when windowOpen resolves true AFTER the generation died (a raced reattach/teardown
  already restored the dock), the stale continuation closes the newly materialized
  vessel by that name before returning — an orphan window can no longer survive the
  race. It touches nothing else.
- reattachAgentDetail: the incremented generation is now CAPTURED, and the
  post-projection continuation revalidates it plus isDestroyed; a destroy (or newer
  operation) landing during the await limits the path to the one vessel cleanup it
  still owns (teardown skipped that close because this reattach had already cleared
  the bookkeeping) — cockpit fields are never resurrected.

Two new witnesses pin both orderings: delayed-open-after-reattach (deferred-open
vessel seam — the popup's Boolean completion arriving after the user reattached)
and destroy-during-reattach (teardown racing the projection await). Fleet battery:
176/176 exit-checked; two-window e2e re-run green (8.2s).

* refactor(agentos): connect window becomes a class-config default — Neo.overwrites-eligible (#14610)

Terminal-gate repair (Euclid's reconciliation): the module-level constant + class-field
alias masked the prototype value Neo.setupClass installs, bypassing Neo.overwrites.
detailVesselConnectWindowMs now lives in static config (documented, non-reactive,
10000ms default) — the timer read and the failed-timeout rollback are unchanged; the
timeout witness passes {detailVesselConnectWindowMs: 20} at creation instead of poking
an internal field post-construct. Fleet battery 176/176 exit-checked; e2e green (6.2s)."
